headline: Cyril Imhof returns repeatedly to agentic AI: its enterprise promise, its security risks, and why building it is harder than conventional software.
body: Imhof's cycle centers on agentic AI adoption: he frames adoption as slow because the technology is immature like early LLMs [4], argues building agents demands a different data-driven mindset than traditional engineering [12], and flags overlooked security threats including prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions as reasons businesses hesitate to deploy [0]. He pushes enterprises to adopt AI or fall behind [8], while weighing infrastructure choices: local LLM deployment for privacy and control over cloud inference [7], avoiding self-built inference as too costly and risky [9], and the question of European cloud sovereignty against US giants [11]. Adjacent threads cover AI-driven high bandwidth memory growth at Micron and SK Hynix [3], smaller agile teams outperforming large ones bogged in coordination overhead [2][6], and AMD competition ending Intel CPU stagnation [5]. Outside the agent thread, Caleb Writes Code gives a visual walkthrough of GPT internals: self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections [10].
